entries.definition:
s–w–ʾ to be or become bad, evil, wicked; to deteriorate; to
afflict, to hurt, to vex, to torment, to trouble, to make sorry. Of
this root, 12 forms occur 167 times in the Qurʾān: سَاءَ saʾa 27
times; سِيءَ sīʾa three times; أَسَاءَ ʾasāʾa five times; سَوْء sawʾ nine
times; سُوء sūʾ once; سَيِّئ sayyiʾ four times; سَيِّئَة sayyiʾatun 22
times; سَيِّئَات sayyiʾāt 36 times; سَوْأَة sawʾatun twice; سَوْآت sawʾāt
five times; السُّوأَى al-sūʾā twice and مُسِيء musīʾ once.

سَاءَ saʾa u I [v. intrans.] this particular form of the verb occurs
only in the perfect and is always used interjectionally in the sense
of ‘How very bad!’, ‘How ill-advised!’, ‘How abominable!’
(16:59) يَتَوَارَى مِنَ الْقَوْمِ مِنْ سُوءِ مَا بُشِّرَ بِهِ أَيُمْسِكُهُ عَلَى هُونٍ أَمْ يَدُسُّهُ فِي التُّرَابِ
أَلَا سَاءَ مَا يَحْكُمُونَ he hides away from the people because of the evil
of what he has been told; should he keep it [the newly born baby
girl] in humiliation (or, and suffer humiliation himself) or bury it
in the dust?, how evil is their judgement! II [v. trans.; pass. سِيءَ
sīʾa] to afflict, to displease, to grieve, to cause agony, to injure
(3:120) إِنْ تَمْسَسْكُمْ حَسَنَةٌ تَسُؤْهُمْ if any goodness comes to you it
grieves them.

أَسَاءَ ʾasāʾa I [v. IV, intrans.] to act badly, to act abominably
(53:31) لِيَجْزِيَ الَّذِينَ أَسَاءُوا بِمَا عَمِلُوا He will repay those who act badly
according to their deeds II [v. trans.] to hurt, to harm, to offend
(30:10) ثُمَّ كَانَ عَاقِبَةَ الَّذِينَ أَسَاءُوا السُّوأَى أَنْ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ then the end of
those who committed the worst abomination was that they denied
God’s revelations.

سَوْء sawʾ [n./ v. n.] 1 evil, corruption, torment (16:60) لِلَّذِينَ لَا
يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ مَثَلُ السَّوْءِ وَلِلَّهِ الْمَثَلُ الْأَعْلَى for those who do not believe in
the Hereafter are evil attributes and for God are the sublime ones
2 harm, injury, damage, destruction (25:40) وَلَقَدْ أَتَوْا عَلَى الْقَرْيَةِ الَّتِي
أُمْطِرَتْ مَطَرَ السَّوْءِ indeed they pass by the town on which destruction
was rained; *(48:6) عَلَيْهِمْ دَائِرَةُ السَّوْءِ upon them is defeat in battle

463                             س/و/أ

[lit. the injurious turn of fortune].

سُوء sūʾ [n.] 1 evil (12:53) إِنَّ النَّفْسَ لَأَمَّارَةٌ بِالسُّوءِ indeed man’s
very soul incites him to evil 2 punishment, harm (13:11) وَإِذَا أَرَادَ
اللَّهُ بِقَوْمٍ سُوءًا فَلَا مَرَدَّ لَهُ and if God wills harm on a people, there is no
turning it back 3 indecency, malice, abuse (12:25) قَالَتْ مَا جَزَاءُ مَنْ
أَرَادَ بِأَهْلِكَ سُوءًا she said, ‘What is a [befitting] punishment for
someone who intended harm to your wife?’ 4 affliction,
corruption, disease (28:32) اسْلُكْ يَدَكَ فِي جَيْبِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضَاءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوءٍ
put your hand inside your garment and it will come out white with
no affliction, [vitiligo] 5 adversity, badness, severity (13:21)
وَيَخَافُونَ سُوءَ الْحِسَابِ and they dread the severity of the Reckoning.

السُّوأَى ʾal-sūʾā [nominalised fem. elat.] the worst there is, the
most abominable thing (30:10) ثُمَّ كَانَ عَاقِبَةَ الَّذِينَ أَسَاءُوا السُّوأَى أَنْ كَذَّبُوا
بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ then the end of those who committed the worst
abomination was that they denied God’s revelations.

سَيِّئ sayyiʾ [quasi-act. part.] bad, malevolent (35:43) اسْتِكْبَارًا فِي
الْأَرْضِ وَمَكْرَ السَّيِّئِ وَلَا يَحِيقُ الْمَكْرُ السَّيِّئُ إِلَّا بِأَهْلِهِ acting arrogantly in the
land, and devising evil–the devising of evil rebounds only on its
own people.

سَيِّئَة sayyiʾatun I [n.; pl. سَيِّئَات sayyiʾāt] 1 sin (2:81) بَلَى مَنْ كَسَبَ
سَيِّئَةً وَأَحَاطَتْ بِهِ خَطِيئَتُهُ truly those who commit a sin
and their sin gains mastery over them–these will be the
inhabitants of the Fire 2 affliction (4:79) وَمَا أَصَابَكَ مِنْ سَيِّئَةٍ فَمِنْ نَفْسِكَ
and whatever affliction befalls you is from your own self 3 bad
deed (23:96) ادْفَعْ بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ السَّيِّئَةَ repel evil/a bad deed with that
which is better 4 punishment (30:36) وَإِنْ تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ
but when punishment befalls them, because of what their
hands have previously committed, they utterly despair 5 bad
outcome, harm, bad result (16:34) فَأَصَابَهُمْ سَيِّئَاتُ مَا عَمِلُوا so the evil
[consequence] of what they had done hit them II [quasi-act. part.]
bad (4:85) وَمَنْ يَشْفَعْ شَفَاعَةً سَيِّئَةً and whoever makes an
intercession [for a bad cause] will have a share of it.

سَوْأَة sawʾatun [n.; pl. سَوْآت sawʾāt] shame, shameful parts,
private parts, nakedness (7:26) يَا بَنِي آدَمَ قَدْ أَنْزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمْ لِبَاسًا يُوَارِي سَوْآتِكُمْ
children of Adam, We have given you garments to cover your

464                             س/و/ح

shameful parts; *(5:31) سَوْأَةَ أَخِيهِ his brother’s exposed dead body.

أَسْوَأ ʾaswaʾ [elat.] worse, worst (39:35) لِيُكَفِّرَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ أَسْوَأَ الَّذِي
عَمِلُوا that God might absolve them [even] of their worst deeds.

مُسِيء musīʾ [act. part.] one who commits bad deeds, evil doer
(40:58) وَمَا يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمَى وَالْبَصِيرُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَلَا الْمُسِيءُ
the blind and the sighted are not equal, any more than those who
believe and do good works and those who do evil.
